GOOG.reasoning: GOOG executed a decisive breakout on 2026-04-30, gapping up from a 4-day consolidation range (~$342–353) on volume of 44.5M vs. 20-day avg 17.1M — a 2.60x ratio, well above the required 1.5x threshold. Price closed at $381.94, a confirmed daily close above resistance. The wiki had been tracking $330.58 as the prior breakout-turned-support and $342–344 as the next target; both are now well exceeded. Range boundaries: consolidation low ~$342, consolidation high ~$353, range height ~$11. Measured move target: $353 + $11 = $364 — already exceeded, suggesting momentum extension toward the Fib 1.272 extension at $412.85. BB width on the daily is 0.2451 and pct_b is 1.136 (price above upper band at $370.90), confirming the squeeze has fully released and volatility is expanding — the coil has fired. Hourly BB is also expanded (pct_b 0.969), confirming hourly breakout alignment. ADX at 63.92 with +DI 50.36 vs -DI 4.4 confirms a very strong trending regime, not a false breakout. Confidence is reduced from full conviction for three reasons: (1) RSI is at 97.75/86.83 — extremely stretched, well beyond normal overbought; (2) the breakout is already 1 day old, and the measured move target of $364 is already exceeded — chasing at $382 means reduced reward-to-risk on the original measured move; (3) the lessons pattern shows a recent NVDA trade (2026-04-30) failed at similarly stretched RSI (81–87) with a stop trigger — the setup structure (high-RSI breakout with no pullback) matches today's GOOG. Hourly volume (0.13x avg) is very low, suggesting the hourly squeeze confirmation is absent at this moment. A pullback toward the breakout level ($353–358) would be a higher-conviction re-entry per the wiki methodology, but the daily breakout itself is valid.

VOTE COUNT: 1 bullish (swing_breakout, 62), 1 bearish (swing_mean_reversion, 52), 3 neutral (swing_trend_momentum 30, swing_catalyst_news 25, swing_macro_context 62). No directional majority. The most striking feature of this panel is that the 3 neutral agents are not confused — they are all saying the same thing: the underlying thesis is valid but the entry is wrong right now. swing_trend_momentum is emphatically bullish on the trend (ADX=63.92, EMA fan textbook) but will not chase — it is waiting for a pullback to EMA10 ~$347-350. swing_macro_context finds the macro regime supportive but the R/R math fails 2:1 at current prices (0.79:1 to nearest support). swing_catalyst_news notes the post-earnings catalyst is already fully priced (the +10% gap-up on Apr 30 is the reaction event, not the setup). Together these three neutral votes form a coherent message: GOOG is the best stock in the basket, it just ran 10% in one day and is not a buyable setup today. The breakout agent is technically correct that a daily close above resistance on 2.6x volume is a valid breakout — but it also acknowledges the measured move target ($364) is already exceeded and RSI is at 97.75, which makes chasing after the fact a low-conviction trade. The mean reversion agent fires the fade structure correctly (Z-score 3.29, pct_b 1.14, both timeframes overbought) but per its own rules requires a reversal candle before entry — none exists. LESSONS APPLIED: The NVDA loss (Apr 30, -$264.65, EMA pullback Fib dip-buy) is a direct pattern-match: high RSI (81-87 on NVDA vs 86-98 on GOOG), no confirmation candle, stretched macro environment. This is the only setup-type in the lessons table and it has a 0% win rate. GOOG's RSI is more extreme than NVDA's at the time of that loss. This dials confidence down on any entry attempt now. WIKI CONTEXT: No open GOOG position (both prior trades abandoned Apr 29). Prior trade #14 was entered at $331.73 on a clean breakout above $330.58 — that structure was similar to today's but the entry was made at the breakout level, not 15% above it. The prior trade hit its $347.50 target. The lesson from prior GOOG history is: enter near the breakout level, not after a gap extension. CONCLUSION: Wait for a pullback. The 3 neutral agents converge on the $342-358 zone as the re-entry window (swing_macro_context: 342-358 for 3:1+ R/R; swing_trend_momentum: EMA10 ~$347-350; swing_breakout: breakout re-test ~$353-358). Any entry in that zone would convert this to a high-conviction long. Today, at $381.94, the trade does not meet the minimum criteria of any strategy agent except swing_breakout — and even that agent acknowledges the original setup has been superseded. Hold cash, set alerts at $353-358 for re-evaluation.
GOOG.key_conflicts: The core conflict is swing_breakout (bullish, 62) vs. swing_mean_reversion (bearish, 52) — one wants to buy the confirmed breakout, the other wants to fade the extreme overbought extension. This conflict is highly informative: it means the trend has transitioned from 'breakout just occurred' to 'breakout is now extended.' swing_breakout is right that the April 30 volume-confirmed daily close is a legitimate breakout event; swing_mean_reversion is right that RSI 97.75 and Z-score 3.29 with no reversal candle are not entry conditions for a new fade either. The resolution: neither side has a clean entry today. swing_trend_momentum and swing_macro_context reinforce this — both are structurally bullish on GOOG but refuse to chase 10% above the EMA. The three neutral agents are not split on direction; they are split on timing. A second conflict worth noting: swing_breakout uses $381.94 as its entry (current market), while swing_macro_context and swing_trend_momentum use $349-355 (pullback zone). This level divergence of ~8% is a red flag — when agents are this far apart on entry, it signals the current price is 'no man's land.' Finally, lessons dial down the sole bullish vote: the NVDA EMA pullback Fib dip-buy setup failed at RSI 81-87, and GOOG's RSI (97.75) exceeds that. A 3-of-5 bullish panel on this setup-type with a 0% lessons win rate would still warrant a neutral call — the actual 1-of-5 bullish reading makes neutral the only defensible outcome.

GOOG.reasoning: TREND ASSESSMENT: Strongly bullish. Daily EMA alignment is a textbook uptrend fan (EMA10=346.87 > EMA21=333.32 > EMA50=320.54), ADX(14)=63.92 (extremely strong), +DI=50.36 vs -DI=4.4 shows dominant directional conviction. Hourly EMAs are identically aligned. Prior wiki read (Apr 15) flagged a breakout above $330.58 resistance with ADX=37 — today's ADX=63.92 confirms that breakout played out powerfully, consistent with prior thesis. MOMENTUM ASSESSMENT: Strongly accelerating. Daily ROC 5d=+13.08%, 10d=+14.78%, 21d=+33.15% — all positive and expanding across longer timeframes, driven by a massive gap-up on 2026-04-30 (+10% on 2.6x average volume). MACD histogram=3.92 (daily) and 3.31 (hourly) both positive, confirming momentum is expanding. OBV trend up, no divergence. OVEREXTENSION / ENTRY RULES VIOLATED: This strategy requires entry on a pullback to the 10 or 21 EMA. Current price ($381.94) is 10.1% above EMA10 and 14.6% above EMA21 — far outside the pullback entry zone. Bollinger %B=1.14 (above upper band), Z-score vs 50-SMA=3.29, RSI(14)=86.83, RSI(7)=97.75. LESSONS APPLIED: The most recent trade lesson (NVDA, 2026-04-30, -$264.65) explicitly flags RSI 81-87 as a failure contributor on a momentum setup — GOOG's RSI is squarely in that same zone. Chasing at current levels replicates the losing setup. MACRO: Risk-on regime (S&P at ATH), supportive but near-term policy uncertainty from Fed chair transition. VERDICT: Trend and momentum are both emphatically bullish, but the entry criteria are not met. Waiting for a pullback to the 10 EMA (~$347-350) or 21 EMA (~$333-335) before triggering. Flagging for wiki curator: prior technicals page warned of overbought at $332; price has since surged to $382, representing a new phase that should update the technicals TL;DR.

1. MACRO REGIME: Risk-on with transitional caveats. S&P 500 at fresh all-time highs above 7,200 (April 2026 best month since 2020). Fed held at 3.5-3.75% on April 29 with 4 dissents (most since Oct 1992) — unusual internal fracture. Chair Powell exits May 15; Kevin Warsh succession introduces hawkish policy uncertainty. Iran peace talks removing the dominant energy/inflation left-tail risk (WTI -2%). Net regime: risk-on but not cleanly — Fed leadership transition and elevated inflation (IMF: 4.4% headline) make this transitional. Alphabet/Amazon Q1 AI cloud beats support the AI capex supercycle narrative. 2. RELATIVE STRENGTH: GOOG is a clear sector outperformer. On April 30, GOOG gapped up strongly (open 371.25 → close 381.94) on 44.5M volume vs 17.1M 20-day avg (2.6x relative volume). ROC: +13.1% (5d), +14.8% (10d), +33.2% (21d). All EMAs aligned in uptrend; price 35-36% above 200-day SMA. This is extreme outperformance — likely post-earnings gap. However, this also flags the trade is LATE in the sector rotation move, not early. 3. ASYMMETRIC R/R — FAILS MINIMUM BAR: Current price ~381.94. Upside: nearest Fib extension 1.272 at 412.85 = +8.1% potential. Downside: nearest volume-confirmed hourly support at 342.69 = -10.3% risk. R/R ratio = 0.79:1 — well below the 2:1 minimum threshold. Even targeting the daily Fib 1.618 extension at 451.29 = +18.2% vs -10.3% downside gives 1.77:1, still below minimum. The stop level that would invalidate relative strength is the hourly support at 342.69, making asymmetry structurally poor at current entry. 4. CAPITAL PRESERVATION FLAGS: RSI 7-day = 97.75, RSI 14-day = 86.83, RSI 21-day = 89.31 — historically extreme overbought levels. Price is above upper Bollinger Band (pct_b = 1.14). Z-score vs 50-period SMA = 3.29 (more than 3 standard deviations above mean). Daily ATR = 2.49% on a post-gap parabolic move creates meaningful gap-fill risk. LESSON FLAG: The recent NVDA trade (-$264.65) was stopped out entering at RSI 81-87 in a stretched macro environment — GOOG's RSI is MORE extreme. Same regime context applies; confidence dialed down accordingly. CONCLUSION: The macro regime supports risk-on longs in AI/cloud. GOOG is demonstrably the strongest name in tech. However, the risk/reward math fails the 2:1 minimum bar at current prices, the RSI is at historic extremes (97.75), and the move is likely post-earnings — meaning the catalyst is already priced. Neutral here; a pullback to the 342-358 zone (hourly Fib 61.8%-78.6% retracement) would create a 3:1+ setup worth entering.

Branch A (Fade the Extreme) fires on GOOG — but without reversal candle confirmation, confidence is capped. Key readings: daily Z-score vs. 50-SMA = 3.29 (well above +2 threshold), daily RSI(14) = 86.83 / RSI(7) = 97.75 (well above 75 overbought level), hourly RSI(21) = 81.05 (overbought on shorter timeframe too). Price is 22.19% above the 50-SMA — extreme extension (>10% qualifies as extreme per strategy). Daily Bollinger pct_b = 1.1363: price trading above the upper band (370.90). Hourly pct_b = 0.9687: pressing against hourly upper band (382.41). Both daily and hourly Z-scores are simultaneously above +2, which per the prompt is the 'strong fade' condition. HOWEVER, the April 30 candle is a large-range gap-up surge closing at the high (open 371.25, close 381.94) — no reversal candle, no doji/hammer/bearish engulfing. No bearish RSI divergence is detected (rsi_divergence.bearish = false). ADX(14) = 63.92 with strong trend and volume 2.6x average — momentum is intact. Strategy rule: WAIT for a reversal candle before entering; do not catch a falling knife. Branch B (Buy the Dip) does not fire: price is at the top of the recent range, not pulling back to Fibonacci support. Lessons context: a recent EMA pullback Fib dip-buy on NVDA at RSI 81-87 with no confirmation candle lost -$264.65 — directly analogous conditions (stretched RSI, no reversal candle). This is a direct pattern match lowering conviction. Wiki context: prior April 15 note flagged GOOG as overbought at $330-332; current price at $381.94 confirms the extension has grown far more extreme since then (current data supersedes wiki). Net: bearish bias is structurally valid given the extreme Z-score/RSI/BB readings on both timeframes, but per-strategy rules require a reversal candle before entry. Confidence capped at 52 given no confirmation candle, strong ADX trend, and recent lesson-pattern match against fading without confirmation.

CATALYST WINDOW: Earnings in 0 trading days — Q1 2026 earnings occurred April 28 post-market (1-2 trading days ago). The post-earnings reaction already happened: GOOG gapped +10% on April 30 (open ~$371, close $381.94) on 2.6x relative volume. No identifiable catalyst within the next ~10 trading days. FOMC was April 29 (passed). DOJ cross-appeal timeline is TBD. NET NEWS SENTIMENT: Mixed/positive overall but catalyst already priced. Positive: antitrust relief (choice-screen, not forced divestiture), Google Cloud AI partnerships, Waymo city expansions, analyst consensus constructive (avg PT $385.46, Citigroup $405). Negative: DOJ cross-appeal for forced divestiture still alive, search index sharing order with Morgan Stanley estimating $15-25B annual ad revenue at risk, massive capex overhang ($175-185B, 2x YoY). INSIDER ACTIVITY: No recent insider trades in the current facts bundle; wiki context notes net -$682M insider flow but characterizes it as routine RSU vesting exercises, not discretionary selling. Net signal: quiet/neutral. TECHNICAL SETUP: Chart is already severely extended post-catalyst. RSI-14 at 86.83, RSI-7 at 97.75 (extreme overbought). Price is 22% above 50 SMA, 36% above 200 SMA, and above the daily Bollinger upper band (pct_b = 1.14). Daily z-score of 3.29. The post-earnings gap has consumed the catalyst thesis. Entry here would be chasing an already-exhausted move with no fresh catalyst on the horizon. LESSONS CHECK: Recent NVDA loss (Apr 30) was triggered precisely by stretched RSI 81-87 with no bullish confirmation — GOOG's RSI is even more extended at 87-98. Per strategy rules: catalyst exists but the technical setup going in is poor (already extended, already ran post-catalyst). Outputting neutral.
